steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says buyers who hadn't previously wanted old action figures suddenly thronged the market. The blank must describe what rising prices did to this demand — demand that did not exist before and then appeared.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — satisfying: satisfying demand means meeting existing desire. These buyers had no prior desire — demand was nonexistent. Opposite of what happened. ✗
  • Choice B — capitalizing: you capitalize on something, not capitalize demand itself. Unidiomatic. ✗
  • Choice C — exploiting: exploiting demand means taking advantage of demand that already exists. This demand was newly created, not pre-existing. ✗
  • Choice D — eliciting: eliciting means drawing out or calling forth something not previously present. Buyers who hadn't wanted the figures before now thronged the market — demand was brought into existence. ✓
That matches Choice D — eliciting.
